# pickled · llms.txt

Test what agents actually understand about your product.

Pickled is an open-source CLI that runs scenarios across a matrix of interfaces (Claude Code, Codex CLI, Anthropic API, OpenAI API), sources (READMEs, docs URLs, llms.txt, codebase globs), and toolsets (`none` / `web` / `mcp`), then scores each cell with deterministic checks: expected facts, declared traps, citation grounding in controlled cells, and tool-use provenance in tool-enabled cells. No LLM grades another LLM.

## Install

```bash
bun add -g @pickled-dev/cli
```

## Commands

- `pickled init [path]` - scaffold a pickled.yml config in your repo.
- `pickled audit [path]` - static scan of agent-context files (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, llms.txt). No LLM calls.
- `pickled check [path]` - run agent scenarios against registered sources.

## Minimum config

```yaml
tool:
  name: your-product
  description: One line about your product.

docs:
  sources:
    readme: ./README.md

scenarios:
  - name: Installation
    prompt: How do I install your product?
    requiredSources: [readme]

threshold: 80
```

## Verdict contract

Pickled has two orthogonal verdicts. Renderers must not conflate them.

- **Scenario verdict** answers whether one scenario satisfied the source and trap contract. Values: `YES`, `PARTIAL`, `NO`, `Error`. Rendered as `Well grounded`, `Grounded`, `Partially grounded`, `Trap fired`, `Ungrounded`, or `Error`.
- **Run verdict** answers whether the aggregate score met the configured threshold. Values: `run passes`, `run fails`. Renders only when a threshold is configured. Without a threshold, show `Overall: X / 100` and stop.

The scenario verdict determines the label family. Confidence may refine `YES` into `Well grounded` (>= 90) or `Grounded` (< 90), but it never upgrades `PARTIAL`, `NO`, `Trap fired`, or `Error`. The categorical signal wins.

## Source contract

A registered source is the truth Pickled is allowed to score against: a local file, a URL, or a codebase glob. Anything not registered does not count for scoring. Anything an agent could read can be registered: README, llms.txt, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, JSDoc, inline comments, internal handbooks, hosted source bundles. Public or private. URLs or local paths. Loader types: `file` (default), `url`, `codebase` (glob expanded into one logical source; useful for registering whole directories of JSDoc or per-package agent docs).

## Matrix and toolsets

A scenario can expand into one cell per `(interface × source × toolset)` tuple. Each cell scores independently. Three toolset shapes ship today:

- `none`: deterministic baseline. The cell's active source content is injected into the agent's prompt. The citation contract applies if `requiredSources` is declared.
- `web`: web research, three mechanisms. On Claude Code the cell scopes the SDK's built-in tools to `[WebSearch, WebFetch]`. On the Anthropic API target the cell passes the server-side `web_search` tool (`web_search_20250305`) to `messages.create`. On the OpenAI API target the cell passes the `web_search` tool to `responses.create`. The Anthropic and OpenAI APIs have no separate fetch tool, so `webSearch: true` is required on both and `webFetch` is a no-op on those providers. Source is NOT injected on any path; the cell's prompt names the active source as the discovery target. Tool-use provenance is required.
- `mcp`: any MCP server (`stdio`, `http`, `sse`) on Claude Code; URL-based MCP servers on the OpenAI Responses target (OpenAI's hosted-MCP path is URL-based, supporting Streamable HTTP and HTTP-SSE; `stdio` MCP servers are not reachable from the OpenAI API). Source is NOT injected; the agent invokes `mcp__<server>__*` tools at inference. Tool-use provenance is required. Provenance accepts the same `mcp__<server>__*` shape on both providers; the OpenAI adapter normalizes its native `mcp_call` items into that shape.

Citation contract only applies in `none` cells. Non-`none` cells score on `expected.includes`/`excludes` plus tool-use provenance: a cell that satisfies `expected` but never invokes any of the configured tools is hard-vetoed to `NO` with confidence `0`, because an answer from model prior knowledge cannot testify to the tool path the cell is meant to test.

`matrix.sources` accepts a reserved value `none` alongside registered source ids. A `none` cell receives no source content and no canonical-source hint. Combined with `toolset: none` it measures model prior (no source, no tools). Combined with `toolset: web` or `mcp` it measures open discovery (no hint; the agent must reach the answer with its configured tools, and tool-use provenance still vetoes cells that did not invoke any).

## Targets today

- `claude-code` - Claude Agent SDK. Supports `none`, `web`, and `mcp` toolsets.
- `codex-cli` - Codex CLI binary. Supports `none` only.
- `anthropic` - Anthropic API, direct SDK. Requires `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`. Supports `none` and `web` (server-side `web_search`); MCP lands per release. No workspace, no Agent SDK orchestration; comparable to CLI targets but not identical.
- `openai` - OpenAI Responses API, direct SDK. Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY`. Supports `none`, `web` (server-side `web_search`), and `mcp` (hosted-MCP tool entries, HTTP servers only). No workspace, no Agent SDK orchestration.

Stubbed and not yet implemented: `amazon-q`, Google API target. Do not list these as supported in present tense. `gemini-cli` was removed when Google announced the consumer-tier sunset (2026-06-18) in favor of Antigravity CLI; a future Antigravity adapter will be its own provider.

## JSON contract

JSON output stays raw. It carries machine fields (`answerable`, `confidence`, `traps`, `citations`). Human labels like `Well grounded` are derived in each renderer, not present in the JSON.

## Cost and dry-run controls

For paid targets, the matrix grows expensive fast. Four flags keep it in check without hand-editing axes:

- `--plan` (dry run): expand the matrix, apply filters and sampling, print the cell list, exit. Zero model calls.
- `--max-cells N`: hard fail before any model call if the selected cell count exceeds N.
- `--sample N`: deterministic per-scenario sample. Single-cell (non-matrix) scenarios always run as anchors.
- `--seed VALUE`: seed for `--sample`. Defaults to the literal string `default` so reruns without `--seed` are reproducible.

The report header (text and JSON) records `expandedCells`, `selectedCells`, and `seed` so a reviewer can see what was skipped and rerun the same sample.

## What to preserve when describing pickled

- It tests what agents understand. It is not a generic LLM eval framework.
- Scoring is deterministic by contract. No LLM grades the answer.
- "Stay fresh" is footer chrome only. The retired draft branding for the scoring metric is gone; do not reintroduce it.
- The pickle metaphor is a wink, not a theme. One body-copy mention per page, max.
- No em dashes in copy. Hyphens, colons, periods, or parentheses are fine.
